Judge: Richard B. Ulmer Jr. Clerk: Robert Woods. Not reported. (302/RBU)

Memorandum Of Points & Authorities In Support Of Motion To Seal Allegations In The Complaint
Off Calendar Jun-08-2023 Text Ruling
MINI MINUTES: No Appearances.

Stockbridge's motion to seal.

The court adopts the tentative ruling as follows RE Stockbridge's motion to seal: Granted and denied in part. Stockbridge seeks to seal the following paragraphs of the complaint: 1, 3, 20, 35, 36, 52, 57, 58, 60, 61, 62, 63, 64, 68, 77, and Exhibit 1. (Movant withdrew its request to seal paragraph 78.) To order sealing, the court must find: (1) There exists an overriding interest that overcomes the right of public access to the record; (2) The overriding interest supports sealing the record; (3) A substantial probability exists that the overriding interest will be prejudiced if the record is not sealed; (4) The proposed sealing is narrowly tailored; and (5) No less restrictive means exist to achieve the overriding interest. (Cal. Rules of Court, rule 2.550(d).) The motion is denied as to paragraphs 1 and 3. Those paragraphs accuse Stockbridge of self-dealing and making misrepresentations. They do not include any confidential/sensitive business information or business methods that a competitor could take advantage of. (Universal City Studios, Inc. v. Superior Court (2003) 110 Cal.App.4th 1273, 1286.) The motion is granted as to paragraphs 20 and 60. The negotiated rate of return is confidential information that a competitor could take advantage of in future developments. (Fancher Decl., par. 10.) The court concludes that there is an overriding interest in sealing this information, Stockbridge will be prejudiced if it is not sealed, and its request is narrowly tailored. The motion is denied as to paragraphs 35, 36, 52, 61, 62, 63, and 64. Stockbridge fails to show what harm could ensue from not redacting the projected investment return information. The motion is denied as to paragraph 57 and 58. There is no overriding interest in sealing the information regarding the prospective investors, one of which was a public investment fund. The motion is denied as to paragraphs 68 and 77. While Stockbridge's valuation of KSWM is confidential financial information, Stockbridge has put the valuation at issue and in the public domain by commencing case CGC-23-605537. The motion is denied as to Exhibit 1. As explained, there is no basis to seal the projected valuations and investor identities.

Judge: Richard B, Ulmer Jr., Clerk: R. Woods, Not Reported. (302/RBU)

KENWOOD INVESTMENTS, LLC'S MOTION TO CONSOLIDATE ACTIONS OF CASE(S) CGC-23-605537 WITH CASE CGC-23-605626.

Ruling - A R G U E D. Upon conclusion of argument, the court adopts the following tentative ruling: Kenwood's motion to consolidate for all purposes is granted. CGC-23-605537 STOCKBRIDGE TREASURE ISLAND INVESTMENT COMPANY, ET AL VS. KENWOOD INVESTMENTS, LLC is consolidated with CGC-23-605626 KENWOOD INVESTMENTS, LLC VS. STOCKBRIDGE CAPITAL PARTNERS, LLC for all purposes. There are overlapping factual and legal issues, no trial dates, and consolidation will conserve court and party resources. The court designates CGC-23-605537 as the lead case.
